verse_specific_anchors: āścaryavat (as a wonder) — the verse's defining adjective
the four-link chain paśyati / vadati / śṛṇoti / veda (sees / speaks / hears / knows)
śrutvāpi ... veda na caiva (even having heard, no one knows) — the gap between hearing and knowing
the trishtubh meter marking the verse out structurally from the surrounding anushtubh shlokas
verdict_reason: The engineering analog anchors specifically on the four-link chain and the gap between hearing and knowing. The phrasing 'Some see this as wonder; some can put part of it into words; the new hire can hear it; and even after hearing it, the new hire does not yet know it' directly mirrors āścaryavat paśyati / vadati / śṛṇoti / śrutvāpi enaṁ veda na caiva kaścit. This four-link structure does NOT appear in 2.26-2.28 or 2.30; it is unique to 2.29 in this block.
